creator: fuz@FreeBSD.org
text:
Recent GnuTLS has a bug where if no system-wide configuration file exists, GnuTLS will refuse to connect to hosts using some normally supported cipher suites.

There's a trivial workaround: install an empty configuration file.

Please update GnuTLS to just ship an empty @sample ${PREFIX}/etc/gnutls/config to
avoid this bug.
